title: cerastium strictum var. suffruticosum
intro: Cerastium strictum var. suffruticosum, also known as dwarf cornflower, is a perennial herb that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and terpenoids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin infections, and digestive problems, due to its potential to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support the immune system and alleviate symptoms of various health conditions.
